title: Survival Course: scRNA‑seq + CITE‑seq
text:
General information

About the course 📌Prerequisites for the CourseAgenda 📆



PRACTICAL MULTILINGUAL ONLINE COURSE
Solving problems focused


Date: 2nd July, 2026 (Thursday)
Meeting Time Conversion:

Mexico City, Mexico

Time zone: GMT‑6
Time: 10:00 AM

Brasília, Brazil

Time zone: GMT‑3
Equivalent time: 01:00 PM


Duration: 3 hours
Level: Intermediate
Register to receive the pre-course instructions: Google form


Instructors:

Thais Crippa de Oliveira, PhD - Posdoctoral Researcher at UNICAMP, Brazil
Evelia Lorena Coss-Navarrete, PhD - Associate Professor at ENES, Juriquilla-UNAM, México. Web page

Assistance:

Juliana Pinto - PhD student, UFPA, Brazil
Adriana da Silva, PhD - Post-doc, UNIFESP, Brazil



🎯 General Goal
Enable participants to navigate a multimodal dataset (RNA + ADT), perform essential preprocessing, and interpret basic results without getting lost in technical details.


✅ Expected Outcomes

Confidence to open and explore multimodal data.
Ability to run a basic Seurat pipeline.
Understanding of how ADT complements RNA.
Awareness of research questions and limitations in CITE‑seq.








Cite and reuse course material
Course data may be freely reused and adapted with proper attribution. All course data from these repositories is subject to the Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0).




Verify that all required R packages are installed, following the instructions provided in Get ready for the workshop.
Ensure you have Git, R, RStudio, and Bash properly installed and working on your computer.
Confirm you have access to a stable internet connection for repository synchronization and package downloads.

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
Bulk RNA‑seq: measures average gene expression in a tissue; useful for detecting global changes, but it hides cellular heterogeneity.

Insufficient for: early developmental studies or complex tissues such as the brain.









Image source: Yan et al. 2024. Int J Biol Sci
crumbs: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
1.2 Single cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq)

First publication: 2009 (Tang et al, 2009)
Popularity: 2014
Estimate the distribution of gene expression levels across a cell population.
Analysis at the single‑cell level.
Reveals cell types, including rare populations.
Analyze temporal dynamics and heterogeneity.








Image source: Single cell RNA sequencing
crumbs: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
section: 1.3 Heterogeneity: Internal diversity within a system or a set
text:
1.3 Heterogeneity: Internal diversity within a system or a set

🔬 Not all cells are the same: even within the same tissue, there are differences in cell types, functional states, and gene expression levels.
🧬 Genetic and transcriptomic variability: each cell may express different genes or varying amounts of RNA, reflecting specific functions.
🩺 Relevance in disease: cellular heterogeneity explains why a tumor, for example, contains subpopulations with distinct behaviors (some more aggressive, others more sensitive to treatment).

Visualization through UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection):







Image source: Explicación completa de un UMAP
crumbs: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
1.4 Questions scRNA‑seq can answer

Discover new or rare cell types.
Identify differential cellular composition between healthy vs. diseased samples.
Understand cell differentiation in development and regeneration.
Analyze plasticity and expression dynamics at the single‑cell level.
Build cellular/genetic atlases → comprehensive catalog of cellular diversity.
Applications in basic research and personalized medicine.








Image source: Kageyama, et al. 2018. Front. Neurosci

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
section: 1.5 General Pipeline for scRNA‑seq with Seurat
text:
1.5 General Pipeline for scRNA‑seq with Seurat








Step
Purpose
Seurat Command





Preprocessing

Load raw count matrix and create Seurat object
Read10X(), CreateSeuratObject()



Quality Control (QC)

Filter cells/genes based on metrics (e.g., nFeature_RNA, percent.mt)
subset(), PercentageFeatureSet()



Normalization

Adjust counts for sequencing depth
NormalizeData() or SCTransform()



Feature Selection

Identify variable genes
FindVariableFeatures()



Dimensionality Reduction

Reduce dimensions for visualization
ScaleData(), RunPCA(), RunUMAP(), RunTSNE()



Clustering & Annotation

Group cells and assign biological meaning
FindNeighbors(), FindClusters(), FindAllMarkers()



Dataset Integration

Combine multiple datasets, remove batch effects
FindIntegrationAnchors(), IntegrateData()



Downstream Analysis

Specific goals: DEGs, trajectories, cell communication
FindMarkers(), CellCycleScoring(), external packages

text:
1.6 Variations of scRNA‑seq







Type of Analysis
Contribution




Unimodal (RNA only)
Cellular heterogeneity in gene expression


Multimodal (RNA + others, e.g., CITE‑seq)
Regulation, proteomics, and biological context


Trajectories
Temporal dynamics of biological processes


Cellular Interactions
Communication and functional networks


Dataset Integration
Comparison across conditions
crumbs: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
1.7 What is CITE-Seq?
CITE‑seq (Cellular Indexing of Transcriptomes and Epitopes by Sequencing) is a multimodal single‑cell technique that combines RNA sequencing with protein profiling at the level of individual cells.

1.7.1 📌 Key points about CITE‑seq

Simultaneous measurements → captures both the transcriptome (scRNA‑seq) and surface proteins using antibody‑derived tags (ADTs).
Highly multiplexed → allows detection of many protein markers in parallel, alongside unbiased RNA profiling.
Single‑cell resolution → thousands of cells can be analyzed at once, integrating RNA and protein data.
Multimodal context → CITE‑seq is part of the broader multimodal analysis frontier in single‑cell genomics, where multiple data types (RNA, ATAC, methylation, perturbations) are measured in the same cell.
Applications → improves cell type identification, refines clustering, and reveals functional states that RNA alone might miss.








Schematic figure of (A) the CITE-seq antibody linked with the barcoded oligo (B) the CITE-seq protocol. Credit: Winston & Gregory Timp (2020), license: CC-BY 4.0

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
1.9 General Pipeline for CITE‑seq








Step
Purpose
Seurat Command





Preprocessing

Load RNA counts and antibody‑derived tags (ADT)
Read10X(), CreateSeuratObject()



Quality Control (QC)

Filter cells/genes based on RNA metrics and ADT counts
PercentageFeatureSet(), subset()



Normalization (RNA)

Adjust RNA counts for sequencing depth
SCTransform() or NormalizeData()



Normalization (ADT)

Center and scale protein counts
NormalizeData(assay = "ADT"), ScaleData(assay = "ADT")



Feature Selection (RNA)

Identify variable genes
FindVariableFeatures()



Dimensionality Reduction

Reduce dimensions for RNA and ADT separately
RunPCA(assay = "RNA"), RunPCA(assay = "ADT")



Multimodal Integration

Combine RNA + ADT signals
FindMultiModalNeighbors(), RunUMAP(nn.name = "weighted.nn")



Clustering & Annotation

Group cells and assign biological meaning
FindClusters(), FindAllMarkers()



Downstream Analysis

Discover enriched proteins per cluster, refine cell types
FindMarkers(assay = "ADT"), external multimodal workflows
crumbs: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq

title: 1  Quick Recap: Introduction to scRNA‑seq + CITE-seq
text:
1.10 scRNA-seq vs scRNA-seq + CITE‑seq








Approach
Type
Purpose




scRNA‑seq
Unimodal (RNA only)
Characterize transcriptomic heterogeneity: identify cell types, states, and gene expression dynamics based solely on RNA.


CITE‑seq
Multimodal (RNA + proteins)
Integrate RNA + surface proteins: refine cell type annotation, validate clusters, and capture functional context beyond transcriptomics.

title: 3  About Dataset
section: 3.2 Paper: An immunophenotype-coupled transcriptomic atlas of human hematopoietic progenitors
text:
3.2 Paper: An immunophenotype-coupled transcriptomic atlas of human hematopoietic progenitors
Nature immunology: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41590-024-01782-4#data-availability 
Raw sequencing FASTQ files and the processed HDF5 (h5) matrix have been deposited in the Gene Expression Omnibus database under accession code GSE245108. CITE-seq data generated by Pei et al. were accessed via the accession ID GSE232559. All imputed Infinity Flow objects are available in the FlowRepository under ID FR-FCM-Z6UQ. An interactive web portal with the associated Azimuth instance and data visualization tools is available at https://altanalyze.org/MarrowAtlas/. Interactive data visualization and analysis tools for the CITE-seq bone marrow single-cell datasets are provided at https://altanalyze.org/MarrowAtlas/. Source data are provided with this paper.


CITE-seq https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE245108
scRNA-seq https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE232559
Github: https://github.com/nsalomonis/Human-Bone-Marrow-Titration-Atlas

NOTAS: ADD image. Info Sobre el dataset (tipo de células o tejidos, Número de células detectadas, tecnología, secuenciador, plataforma, genoma usado para el alineamiento, versión de cell ranger) (D5 https://eveliacoss.github.io/Workshop_scRNAseq_presentacion/Clase2_Normalizacion.html#5 )

title: 4  Single-Cell Survival Course with Seurat v5
text:
HOW TO USE
Run interactively, section by section (Ctrl+Enter / Cmd+Enter), from top
to bottom. Do NOT source() the whole file in one go: several chunks are
deliberate errors meant to be read, and Block 8 is optional take-home
work that depends on a file released separately after the session.
DELIBERATE ERRORS
Five steps are designed to fail (v4 slot access, GetAssayData slot=,
wrong filter thresholds, dims > npcs, plotting a label before adding it).
They are wrapped in try() so the error prints to the console without
halting a full run. Read the message, then continue.
WORKING DIRECTORY
Set the working directory to the folder holding this script and the data/
subfolder (Session > Set Working Directory > To Source File Location).
